title: ceropegia choanantha
intro: Ceropegia choanantha, a species of flowering plant in the milkweed family, is native to India and is also known as the 'Flame Flower' or 'Coral Flower' due to its vibrant, orange-red and white coloration. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat respiratory issues and as an anti-inflammatory agent. The health benefits of Ceropegia choanantha include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive problems, and act as an expectorant to relieve coughs and colds. Ceropegia choanantha can be prepared as an herbal tea, decoction, or powder, making it a versatile remedy in traditional medicine.
